question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: ToBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the genesis block path in ToBlock() (where rb.Index == genesisBlock.Index) by finding a non-genesis block that happens to have the same index as the cached genesisBlock, causing ConsensusStartNanos to be overridden with genesisBlock.ConsensusStart and producing a block time delta that exceeds 500% of the 24-hour average?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: selectGenesis] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the join condition 'on rf.consensus_end > genesis.timestamp' in selectGenesis to cause the system to select a genesis block that is far in the past (with a very small consensus_end), making the genesis ConsensusStart so early that all subsequent block time calculations show a 500%+ delay relative to the 24-hour average?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: findBlockByHash()] Can an unprivileged external user send thousands of concurrent FindByHash() requests with random hashes, causing the selectByHashWithIndex query to saturate DB connection pools, delaying legitimate block queries and making the apparent time since the last block exceed 500% of the 24-hour average?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: selectLatestWithIndex] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of a WHERE clause in selectLatestWithIndex (it scans all record_file rows ordered by index desc) to cause a slow query when the record_file table is large, delaying the response long enough that the wall-clock time since the last reported block exceeds 500% of the 24-hour average?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: initGenesisRecordFile()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the condition 'if br.genesisBlock.ConsensusStart != genesisConsensusStartUnset' in initGenesisRecordFile() — where genesisConsensusStartUnset = -1 — by causing a scenario where a legitimate genesis block has ConsensusStart = -1 (e.g., due to data corruption), preventing genesis initialization and making all block time queries fail indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: RetrieveLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the check 'if rb.Index < br.genesisBlock.Index' in RetrieveLatest() — where a cached genesisBlock.Index is very high — to cause all current blocks to be reported as ErrBlockNotFound, making the network appear to have no valid blocks and simulating a complete transaction freeze?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: selectGenesis] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 'limit 1' in the genesis CTE subquery ('select consensus_timestamp from account_balance where account_id = @treasury_entity_id order by consensus_timestamp limit 1') to cause the system to use the earliest possible treasury balance timestamp as genesis, which if anomalously old, makes the genesis block's ConsensusStart so early that block time deltas appear to be 500%+ of the 24-hour average?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: findBlockByIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user call FindByIndex() with index values that correspond to gaps in the record_file table (missing block indices), causing repeated ErrBlockNotFound responses that, when observed by Rosetta clients, appear as a sequence of missing blocks indicating a block time delay of 500%+ of the 24-hour average?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: selectByHashWithIndex] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the correlated subquery in selectByHashWithIndex ('select c.consensus_start - 1 from record_file c where c.index = p.index + 1') to cause a scenario where the subquery returns a consensus_start - 1 value that is less than the block's own consensus_start, producing a negative or zero block duration that confuses block time averaging and masks a 500%+ delay?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: RetrieveLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the inconsistency between selectLatestWithIndex (which returns raw consensus_end) and selectByHashWithIndex/selectRecordBlockByIndex (which use COALESCE to adjust consensus_end), causing the same block to report different ConsensusEndNanos depending on which query path is used, and making block time calculations inconsistent enough to appear as a 500%+ delay?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: GetTimestampMillis()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where ConsensusStartNanos is set to a value that, when divided by int64(time.Millisecond) in GetTimestampMillis(), produces a timestamp far in the past due to integer truncation, causing Rosetta clients to compute a block age of 500%+ of the 24-hour average and halt transaction submission?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: initGenesisRecordFile()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that keep the genesis initialization in a perpetual retry loop (by causing the selectGenesis query to always return ErrNodeIsStarting via DB errors), preventing the genesis block from ever being cached and making all block time queries fail, simulating a complete network freeze?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: selectGenesis] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that selectGenesis uses 'coalesce((select rf1.consensus_start-1 from record_file rf1 where rf1.index = rf.index + 1), consensus_end)' for the genesis block's consensus_end, and if the genesis block is the only block in record_file, its consensus_end equals the raw stored value, which could be anomalously large and make the genesis block span 500%+ of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: FindByIdentifier()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the two-step lookup in FindByIdentifier() (first findBlockByHash, then index comparison) to cause a TOCTOU condition where the block's index changes between the hash lookup and the index check, resulting in ErrBlockNotFound for a valid block and making the network appear to have a missing block that implies a 500%+ block time delay?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: RetrieveLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of rate limiting on RetrieveLatest() calls to perform a timing attack that measures the DB query latency of selectLatestWithIndex, inferring when the latest block's ConsensusEndNanos is anomalously large (indicating a 500%+ block time delay) before legitimate monitoring systems detect it?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: findBlockByHash()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a hash that matches a block with a very large ConsensusEndNanos (due to COALESCE fallback) via FindByHash(), causing the returned block to appear to span 500%+ of the 24-hour average block time, and use this to convince Rosetta API consumers that the network is frozen?
